title: Planning, Dependencies, and Adaptive Decomposition · Control-Loop Lens: Replanning
summary: Plan a migration whose dependencies and risks emerge during work. Planned artifact: Add and verify the loops replanning increment in a dependency-aware adaptive execution plan. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Update the plan when evidence changes while preserving the goal and recorded decisions.
Choose milestones that leave a verified, restartable artifact.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

options.text: Update the plan when evidence changes while preserving the goal and recorded decisions. Apply it to the concrete input and state the observable result.
Changing a plan means the original work failed. This does not resolve predict replanning.

For predict replanning, apply Replanning without its stated preconditions, omit the a dependency-aware adaptive execution plan trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Replanning for a dependency-aware adaptive execution plan.

steps.title: Build Replanning evidence

Done when: [replace with changed-case acceptance criteria]
